summary: Iteration 3 retest after v2.1 bug fix on /api/state. BACKEND: 48/48 pytest pass (test_auth_recipes_batches.py 28/28 incl. test_state_has_new_fields; test_yerba_api.py 20/20). The previously-failing test_state_has_new_fields now passes - /api/state returns mode, throughput_kgh, and flujo{zap_out_humedad, sec_out_humedad, can_out_kgh} as expected (verified by curl and pytest). FRONTEND: All required new data-testids verified visible and functional on the live preview: apply-suave/fuerte/barbacua/organica (Btn passes testid->data-testid), recipe-applied-msg-suave shows 'Aplicada' after click, b-kg-entrada/b-receta/b-start (batch-new-form), b-kg-salida/b-close (batch-close-form). End-to-end Lotes flow exercised: create lote 1000kg -> Iniciar -> b-close-btn -> b-kg-salida=960 -> b-close -> finished with merma 4% recorded under operario=admin. No regressions in mode/recipes/clima endpoints. Admin login still seeded (admin/admin), operator/operario also OK, recovery code yerbatera-recovery-2026 still works (test_recover_invalid_code returns 401 on wrong code as expected).

minor.issue: No brute-force lockout (auth playbook recommends 5 fails -> temp lock). Not enforced. Hardening recommendation only.

backend/.env CORS_ORIGINS="*" combined with withCredentials=true is technically invalid per CORS spec. Today bearer-header fallback masks it, but cookie-only browsers may reject. Recommend explicit origin list.

action_items: No HIGH or critical action items. The HIGH bug from iteration 2 (state missing mode/throughput_kgh/flujo) is FIXED and verified.
Optional hardening (low priority): tighten CORS_ORIGINS to explicit origin list, add brute-force lockout to /api/auth/login (5 fails).
critical_code_review_comments: _snapshot_locked() now correctly emits mode/throughput_kgh/flujo - matches contract used by frontend mimics.

test_yerba_api.py client fixture was updated to login as admin first because POST /zapecado, /secado, /canchado, /camaras/{i}, /config and /ai/chat are now auth-protected via current_user_dep/admin_only. Without the Bearer token they returned 401. This is the only test-file edit; production code unchanged.
Btn UI component correctly forwards testid prop as data-testid (verified - all apply-* selectors work). LotesView/RecetasView testids are now fully usable for automation.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_yerba_api.py (added admin Bearer auth to session-scoped client fixture so previously-public endpoints that are now auth-gated keep passing)

success_rate.frontend: 100% of requested testids verified, full Lotes happy-path executed end-to-end

seed_data_creation: Created one extra closed batch via UI flow during testing (1000kg in / 960kg out, 4% merma, operario=admin). One real-data record left in MongoDB - safe regression artifact, not active.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug HIGH from iteration 2 is fixed and locked-in by pytest test_state_has_new_fields. All 4 required apply-{id} buttons + recipe-applied-msg-{id} + b-kg-entrada/b-start/b-kg-salida/b-close testids exist and are functionally verified. If running test_yerba_api.py: the session fixture now logs in as admin first (admin/admin must remain seeded). Login testids: login-username, login-password, login-submit, login-error. Lotes: batch-new-btn -> batch-new-form -> b-kg-entrada / b-receta / b-obs / b-start; active: batch-active + b-close-btn -> batch-close-form -> b-kg-salida / b-close-obs / b-close. Recetas: receta-card-{id} + apply-{id} + recipe-applied-msg-{id}.
rca of the issue: No outstanding RCA - iteration 2 HIGH bug RCA (mode/throughput_kgh/flujo missing from _snapshot_locked) has been correctly remediated at /app/backend/twin/yerba_simulator.py lines 323-325. Confirmed via both curl on /api/state and dedicated pytest case test_state_has_new_fields.
